attractions.name: Juneau International Airport
attractions.season: Year-round; fog and wind cancellations Oct-Mar
attractions.blurb: Jet gateway to Juneau, Glacier Bay and the northern panhandle - no road connects Juneau to anywhere.
attractions.tip: Juneau flights divert in fog. Never book a same-day cruise, ferry or Gustavus connection off a JNU arrival.

attractions.name: Sitka Rocky Gutierrez Airport
attractions.season: Year-round jet service
attractions.blurb: Jet field on Japonski Island, a 10-minute walk from downtown Sitka across the O'Connell Bridge.
attractions.tip: The only Southeast airport you can walk to town from - skip the rental car for a two-day Sitka stop.

attractions.name: Kodiak Benny Benson State Airport
attractions.season: Year-round; frequent weather cancellations
attractions.blurb: Jet field 5 mi south of Kodiak town, shared with USCG Base Kodiak and the floatplanes to the bear refuge.
attractions.tip: Kodiak weather scrubs flights often - build a spare day, or use the Homer-Kodiak ferry as your backup.

attractions.name: Ralph Wien Memorial Airport, Kotzebue
attractions.season: Year-round jet service from Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Jet gateway above the Arctic Circle and the charter base for Kobuk Valley, Noatak and Cape Krusenstern.
attractions.tip: No roads leave Kotzebue - every park visit needs a separate air-taxi charter booked months ahead.

attractions.name: Nome Airport
attractions.season: Year-round jet service from Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Jet field serving Nome and its 250 mi of gravel roads - the rare Alaska bush hub with rental cars.
attractions.tip: Reserve a Nome rental months out; the fleet is tiny and sells out for Iditarod week and June birding.

attractions.name: Bethel Airport
attractions.season: Year-round jet service from Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Busiest bush hub in western Alaska; springboard to 50+ Yup'ik villages and the Yukon Delta refuge.
attractions.tip: Bethel is a working hub, not a tourist town - lodging is scarce and pricey, so book before you fly.

attractions.name: King Salmon Airport
attractions.season: Jets Jun-Sept; year-round limited service
attractions.blurb: Old Air Force field that is the jet gateway to Katmai; floatplanes hop 30 mi from here to Brooks Camp.
attractions.tip: Same-day Anchorage-King Salmon-Brooks is weather-fragile; plan a King Salmon overnight as your buffer.

attractions.name: Tom Madsen Airport, Unalaska/Dutch Harbor
attractions.season: Year-round; among the most weather-cancelled fields in the US
attractions.blurb: Short 3,900 ft runway boxed in by mountains and water; landings are hand-flown and often turned back.
attractions.tip: Budget two spare days each way, or ride the M/V Tustumena one direction - Dutch flights routinely cancel.

attractions.name: Wiley Post-Will Rogers Memorial Airport, Utqiagvik
attractions.season: Year-round; two Alaska Airlines flights daily
attractions.blurb: Northernmost jet airport in the United States, on the gravel edge of the Arctic Ocean at Utqiagvik.
attractions.tip: Book a guided package - independent arrivals find no taxis, no rental cars and no signage in town.

attractions.name: Merle K. (Mudhole) Smith Airport, Cordova
attractions.season: Year-round jet service
attractions.blurb: Jet stop 13 mi from Cordova on the Copper River Highway; the town's lifeline alongside the ferry.
attractions.tip: Pre-book the airport shuttle or a rental - there is no taxi rank and it is a 13 mi walk into town.

attractions.name: Yakutat Airport
attractions.season: Year-round; one flight each way per day
attractions.cost: Jet fare on the Juneau-Anchorage milk run
attractions.blurb: WWII-era runway on the Gulf coast; the only scheduled way in to Hubbard Glacier trips and Yakutat surf.
attractions.tip: One flight a day each way - a missed connection or a weather scrub costs you a full 24 hours.

attractions.name: Lake Hood Seaplane Base
attractions.season: Floats May-Oct, skis Dec-Mar; ice-up break in between
attractions.cost: Free to watch; charters $400+ per seat
attractions.blurb: World's busiest floatplane base beside ANC; most Anchorage bear-viewing and flightseeing trips leave here.
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 min early and watch takeoffs from the gravel loop road - free, and the best plane-spotting in Alaska.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr of watching, or the departure point for a full-day charter

attractions.name: Deadhorse Airport
attractions.season: Year-round; daily jets serving the oilfield
attractions.blurb: Jet field at Prudhoe Bay; the standard Dalton Highway plan is drive north one way and fly back from here.
attractions.tip: You cannot reach the actual Arctic Ocean without a security-cleared oilfield tour booked 24 hours ahead.
attractions.duration_note: the exit door of a 3-day Dalton drive

attractions.name: Bellingham Cruise Terminal
attractions.aka: AMHS southern terminus, Fairhaven Terminal, Bellingham WA
attractions.season: Weekly sailings year-round; summer books out first
attractions.cost: ~$250 walk-on to Ketchikan; vehicles and cabins far more
attractions.blurb: Where the Alaska Marine Highway starts: a 38-hour Friday sailing from Washington to Ketchikan.
attractions.tip: Cabins and vehicle space vanish the day AMHS releases schedules (usually late fall for next summer).
attractions.duration_note: 2 nights aboard to Ketchikan, 3 to Juneau

attractions.name: Skagway Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round; more frequent May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Northern end of the AMHS mainline, a five-minute walk from Broadway and the White Pass depot.
attractions.tip: The private Haines-Skagway fast ferry runs far more often than AMHS and takes only 45 minutes.

attractions.name: Sitka Ferry Terminal
attractions.aka: AMHS Sitka, Halibut Point Road Mile 7
attractions.season: Year-round, a few sailings per week
attractions.blurb: AMHS dock 7 mi north of town with no bus service - a genuine trap for walk-on foot passengers.
attractions.tip: Pre-arrange a shuttle or taxi. Ferries often land at 2am and nothing meets the boat.

attractions.name: Cordova Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Sparse year-round schedule; best May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Downtown AMHS dock; the Whittier and Valdez runs are Cordova's only surface link to the road system.
attractions.tip: Sailings run a few times a week and skip whole stretches - check AMHS before planning any Sound loop.

attractions.name: Petersburg Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round mainline stop
attractions.blurb: Mile 0.9 Mitkof Highway; mainline ferries time the Wrangell Narrows tide to reach this dock.
attractions.tip: Ride the Wrangell-Petersburg leg in daylight - 22 mi of buoy-lined narrows is the best deck time in Alaska.
attractions.duration_note: transit point; 3 hr scenic leg from Wrangell

attractions.name: Alaska Public Lands Information Center - Anchorage
attractions.season: Year-round; long summer hours, reduced Oct-Apr
attractions.blurb: One desk covering every NPS, USFS, BLM, refuge and state unit in Alaska - maps, permits, ranger advice.
attractions.tip: Make it your day-one stop: bear-viewing and public-use-cabin advice you cannot get from any one website.
attractions.duration_note: 45 min downtown, easy walk from the depot

attractions.name: Talkeetna Ranger Station
attractions.season: Daily mid-Apr to early Sept; limited winter hours
attractions.blurb: NPS station where every Denali and Foraker climber registers, 60 days ahead, before flying to base camp.
attractions.tip: Non-climbers should still stop for the free daily ranger talk and the room-sized Alaska Range relief map.

attractions.name: Glacier Bay Visitor Information Station
attractions.season: Late May to early Sept
attractions.blurb: Dockside station issuing camping and private-boat permits and running the required kayaker orientation.
attractions.tip: Backcountry campers must attend the in-person orientation here - it cannot be done online or by phone.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr, do it the afternoon before you launch

attractions.name: Bettles Ranger Station
attractions.aka: Gates of the Arctic ranger station, Bettles Field
attractions.season: Summer staffed; winter by appointment
attractions.blurb: Main staging station for Gates of the Arctic: bear-can loan, route advice and the backcountry orientation.
attractions.tip: Air taxis stage from Bettles Field next door - charter seats and float dates book up by February.
attractions.duration_note: half-day of staging before a multi-day trip

attractions.name: Denali Park Road Closure - East Fork Bridge (Mile 43)
attractions.aka: Pretty Rocks landslide, Polychrome Area Project
attractions.season: Closure in effect through summer 2026 - verify with NPS
attractions.blurb: The Pretty Rocks landslide closes the Park Road at Mile 43; every bus and vehicle turns around here.
attractions.tip: Eielson VC, Wonder Lake and Kantishna stay unreachable through summer 2026 - check nps.gov before booking.
attractions.duration_note: the turnaround on a 7-8 hr bus day

attractions.name: Teklanika River Campground
attractions.season: Late May to early Sept
attractions.blurb: The only Denali campground you may drive your own vehicle to past Savage River, at Park Road Mile 29.
attractions.tip: Three-night minimum and the car stays parked until you leave; the Tek Pass then lets you re-ride buses free.

attractions.name: Riley Creek Campground
attractions.season: Open year-round; services late May-Sept only
attractions.blurb: Denali's big entrance campground, walking distance from the bus depot, mercantile and Riley Creek trails.
attractions.tip: Reserve by March for July; otherwise show up at the mercantile at 8am for same-day walk-in tent sites.

attractions.name: Brooks Camp Campground
attractions.season: June 1 - Sept 17
attractions.cost: ~$18 per person per night, plus ~$800 floatplane
attractions.blurb: Electric-fenced 60-person campground at Brooks Falls - the only affordable way to sleep at Katmai's bear river.
attractions.tip: Reservations open on recreation.gov in early January; peak July nights are gone within minutes.
attractions.duration_note: 2 nights buys you the falls at dawn and dusk

attractions.name: Byers Lake Campground & Kesugi Ridge Trailhead
attractions.aka: Denali State Park, Parks Highway Mile 147
attractions.season: Campground late May-Sept; ridge snow-free Jul-Sept
attractions.blurb: Lakeside Denali State Park campground and the south end of the Kesugi Ridge traverse, facing Denali.
attractions.tip: Kesugi Ridge is a 27 mi point-to-point; day-hike the Cascade Trail up to the ridge for the same view.
attractions.duration_note: day hike, or 3 days end-to-end on the ridge

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Fairbanks Depot
attractions.season: Denali Star mid-May to mid-Sept; Aurora Winter Sept-May
attractions.blurb: Northern terminus of the Denali Star and the weekends-only Aurora Winter Train, north of downtown.
attractions.tip: The Aurora Winter Train runs weekends mid-Sept to mid-May - a 12-hr daylight ride through frozen Interior.

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Talkeetna Depot
attractions.season: Denali Star mid-May to mid-Sept; Hurricane Turn Thu-Sun
attractions.blurb: Denali Star stop and the boarding point for the Hurricane Turn, America's last scheduled flag-stop train.
attractions.tip: Hurricane Turn runs Thu-Sun in summer; anywhere along the line you can flag it down by waving a white cloth.
attractions.duration_note: transit point; Hurricane Turn is a 6 hr round trip

attractions.name: Spencer Glacier Whistle Stop
attractions.aka: Glacier Discovery train stop, Spencer Lake
attractions.season: Train runs mid-May to mid-Sept only
attractions.blurb: Rail-only stop on the Glacier Discovery train; 1.3 mi of trail to an iceberg lake below Spencer Glacier.
attractions.tip: The train gives you about 4 hours on the ground - the float back down the Placer River must be booked ahead.

attractions.name: Maclaren River Lodge
attractions.season: Full service summer; winter snowmachine access only
attractions.blurb: Roadhouse at the Maclaren River bridge - the only dependable fuel, food and beds on the 135 mi Denali Highway.
attractions.tip: Fill up in Cantwell or Glennallen anyway; lodge fuel is expensive and can run dry mid-season.
attractions.duration_note: a meal stop, or an overnight mid-highway

attractions.name: Paxson Junction
attractions.aka: Richardson Highway Mile 185, Denali Highway Mile 0
attractions.season: Junction open year-round; Denali Hwy closed Oct-mid-May
attractions.blurb: Where the Denali Highway meets the Richardson - and where the lodge closed, leaving no fuel, food or lodging.
attractions.tip: Nearest dependable gas is Glennallen 70 mi south or Delta Junction 80 mi north; carry 200 mi of range.
attractions.duration_note: a decision point, not a stop

attractions.name: Anchor Point Beach - Westernmost Highway Point in North America
attractions.season: Road open year-round; tractor launches May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Sign at the end of Anchor River Beach Road marking the farthest west you can drive on a connected highway.
attractions.tip: Tractors push halibut boats straight off the sand at high tide - worth the 2 mi detour off the Sterling Hwy.
attractions.duration_note: 30-60 min detour en route to Homer

attractions.name: Cordova
attractions.season: Year-round; shorebirds May, salmon Jun-Aug, ski Feb-Apr
attractions.cost: Free (town); ferry or jet to reach
attractions.blurb: Fishing town of 2,600 with no road out: ferry or jet only, and the gateway to the Copper River Delta.
attractions.tip: Rent a truck on arrival - the Copper River Highway and Alaganik Slough are the reason to come, 20-36 mi out.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 days, longer if the ferry schedule dictates

attractions.name: Craig
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Year-round; road network best May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Largest town on Prince of Wales Island and the service hub for its 1,500 mi of paved and logging roads.
attractions.tip: Last reliable fuel, groceries and tire repair on the island - top up before running out to El Capitan Cave.
attractions.duration_note: overnight base for a 2-3 day island loop

attractions.name: Midnight Sun Baseball Game
attractions.season: One night only, on or about June 21
attractions.blurb: Goldpanners play from 10pm past midnight on the solstice with no artificial lights - a tradition since 1906.
attractions.tip: Tickets go on sale in spring and sell out; the point is the innings after midnight, so do not leave early.
attractions.duration_note: one late evening at Growden Memorial Park

attractions.name: Kodiak Crab Festival
attractions.season: Memorial Day weekend, late May
attractions.cost: Free entry; food and rides extra
attractions.blurb: Memorial Day street fair on Kodiak's waterfront: survival-suit races, crab feed and blessing of the fleet.
attractions.tip: Flights and rooms fill for the whole weekend - book by March if you want to be in town for it.
attractions.duration_note: one full day of the 4-day event

attractions.name: Little Norway Festival
attractions.season: Third full weekend of May
attractions.blurb: Petersburg's Norwegian-heritage weekend: bunads, a Viking longship, valkyries and a beer-batter halibut feed.
attractions.tip: The ferry is the cheapest way in; Petersburg's handful of inns book out months before the third May weekend.

attractions.name: Girdwood Forest Fair
attractions.season: First full weekend of July
attractions.blurb: Three-day artisan fair in the Girdwood rainforest - no corporate booths and no politics, by charter since 1976.
attractions.tip: Parking is hopeless; take the free shuttle or ride the bike path from Alyeska. Cash for most food booths.

attractions.name: Salmonfest
attractions.season: Late July / early August (Jul 31-Aug 2 in 2026)
attractions.blurb: Three-day music, fish and salmon-politics festival on the Ninilchik fairgrounds - Alaska's biggest concert.
attractions.tip: Camp on site. The Sterling Highway back to Homer or Soldotna after a late set is not a drive you want.

attractions.name: Copper River Delta Shorebird Festival
attractions.season: First full weekend of May
attractions.blurb: Cordova festival timed to millions of shorebirds staging on the Copper River Delta mudflats each spring.
attractions.tip: The migration peaks inside a 10-day window in early May; Alaganik Slough boardwalk is the best viewing.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 days, plus ferry or flight time

attractions.name: Sitka WhaleFest
attractions.season: Early November, usually the first weekend
attractions.blurb: November marine-science symposium plus boat trips onto Sitka Sound when humpbacks bubble-net feed in numbers.
attractions.tip: The whale-watch boats sell out first. Pack for honest Southeast rain - November is the wettest month.

attractions.name: Alaska Folk Festival
attractions.season: One week in April, around the second Monday
attractions.blurb: Free week-long acoustic music festival at Juneau's Centennial Hall: no headliners, 15-minute sets, all volunteer.
attractions.tip: Admission is free but Juneau's hotels fill with musicians - book a room before the schedule is announced.
attractions.duration_note: an evening, or the whole week

attractions.name: Alaska Bald Eagle Festival
attractions.season: Mid-November, at the peak of the eagle gathering
attractions.blurb: Haines festival held when thousands of bald eagles concentrate on the Chilkat's late, ice-free chum run.
attractions.tip: Eagle numbers peak roughly Nov 5-25; the Council Grounds at Haines Highway Mile 19 is the viewing spot.
